opp.name: BauschHealth [S2P] Oct'25
opp.next_step: June 15 - They have a long timeline. They still need to meet with European mgmt to see if they can move towards a formal project
opp.ais_why:
• Anthony Gambino opened the Oct 15 intro saying he wanted 'tactical AI to automate tail-end spend' and asked specifically about Anna for low-value continuous savings.
• The April 22 deeper-dive brought three Bausch IT/procurement leaders into a Merlin Intake and Anna walkthrough, with the prospect debriefing internally on the agentic story.
• Two recorded calls six months apart, plus a follow-up scheduled for May 13, signal sustained AI engagement rather than a one-off curiosity.

My leadership is gonna look for how much is the system

items.area: Quantified baseline metrics (tail-spend leakage, cycle time, headcount burden)
items.quote: How long is it gonna take to implement, and then what's the ROI on investment, and then it's gonna change the way we work, and how does that fit here?

sources: Avoma notes 15 October 2025: ROI expectations, challenges, key takeaways
metrics.narrative: Buyer articulated operational pain around tail-spend inefficiency during the 15 October 2025 introductory call, specifically citing the need to aggregate low-volume purchases and negotiate bulk pricing (e.g. 'saving $100 on 50 mice' via the Anna module, per Zycus demo). Anthony Gambino asked about ROI timeline and fit: 'How long is it gonna take to implement, and then what's the ROI on investment, and then it's gonna change the way we work, and how does that fit here?' (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). Buyer requested benchmarks and ROI case studies from similar pharmaceutical customers to validate system cost, timelines, and tangible benefits (source: Avoma notes 15 Oct 2025). However, no quantified baseline metrics were provided by the buyer (current tail-spend leakage, procurement cycle time, headcount savings target, or annual addressable spend). The business case remains unbuilt. Zycus estimated licensing cost at ~$500k/year plus implementation; buyer needs a 3-year TCO breakdown to evaluate (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). Gap: quantifying the buyer's current-state pain and building a written ROI model is the critical next step to move from investigation to formal project.

sources: Avoma call 15 October 2025: only Anthony Gambino attended
OpportunityContactRole: Lenora Bell listed, no engagement record
Next_Step__c 15 June 2026: no buyer reply to rep outreach
champion.narrative: No champion has been developed. Anthony Gambino was the sole buyer participant on the 15 October 2025 introductory call and demonstrated interest (asked substantive ROI, fit, and timeline questions), but he has not re-engaged in 8+ months and holds no budget authority (he defers to 'leadership' and European management per the call). Lenora Bell (Procurement Manager) is the only buyer-side OpportunityContactRole on record but has not appeared on any call or in Next Steps; her engagement level is unknown. No coach, internal advocate, or access to the Economic Buyer / CFO / European management decision-makers has been established. The deal is single-threaded through Gambino, who is now unresponsive. Gap: the deal owner must re-engage Gambino, multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder (Director US Indirect Sourcing) or other senior procurement/finance stakeholders, and build a champion with access to the EB and decision process.

sources: Avoma call 15 October 2025, Anthony Gambino quote
Avoma notes: key takeaways re investigation stage and tooling
competition.narrative: Buyer is benchmarking Zycus against two incumbent alternatives before committing to a new platform: ServiceNow and SAP. Anthony Gambino stated on the 15 October 2025 call: 'They're gonna look to ServiceNow, and they're gonna look to SAP to say, well, what do you guys have before I go bolt on something new?' (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). This positions ServiceNow as the primary current threat (active evaluation, likely tied to existing IT/service-management footprint) and SAP as a secondary incumbent (ERP-adjacent procurement tools). No external procurement-platform competitors (Coupa, Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP) have been named. The buyer is also evaluating a do-nothing / defer scenario ('no formal budget or project has been defined yet' per Avoma notes 15 Oct 2025). Wedge to win: demonstrate that Merlin AI / agentic intake automation delivers ROI and workflow fit that ServiceNow and SAP cannot match, anchored to tail-spend use cases and pharma-customer benchmarks. The buyer requested benchmarks and ROI case studies from similar pharmaceutical customers (source: Avoma notes 15 Oct 2025); Zycus promised to send a deck but delivery is unconfirmed. The competitive read is 8 months stale; the deal owner must re-engage to learn whether ServiceNow or SAP have since proposed solutions.

sources: Avoma call 15 October 2025, Anthony Gambino engagement and questions
identify_pain.narrative: Buyer confirmed operational pain around tail-spend inefficiency and the need to automate low-volume, high-touch procurement on the 15 October 2025 call. Anthony Gambino engaged on the Anna module demo, which automates tail spend by aggregating purchases and negotiating bulk pricing (example: 'saving $100 on 50 mice' for lab supplies). He also cited the need to assess 'how the system will change the way we work' and whether it fits current processes (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). The pain is confirmed but not quantified (no baseline spend leakage, cycle time, or headcount burden provided). Pain owner: Anthony Gambino (Supply Chain / Procurement Manager) per the call; broader procurement leadership (Melissa Simpson, Director US Indirect Sourcing per account contacts) likely co-owns the pain but has not been engaged. The buyer is investigating whether to address the pain via a new platform (Zycus) or via existing ServiceNow/SAP tools, signaling the do-nothing/incumbent alternative is live.

sources: Q2/Q3 SOQL reads: no Paper Process, Mandate, or contracting fields populated
Avoma call 15 October 2025: no legal/InfoSec discussion
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, or InfoSec process has been discussed. The deal is pre-project; no MSA, SOW, or Order Form mechanics have surfaced. Given Bausch Health's pharma/life-sciences sector, expect InfoSec review, data-privacy (GDPR/HIPAA-adjacent), and ERP integration diligence before contracting. No SI/partner involvement mentioned. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date is on record. Gap: paper-process visibility will emerge only after the buyer formalizes the project and enters RFP or vendor-selection stage.

sources: Avoma call 15 October 2025, Anthony Gambino quote
OpportunityContactRole: only Lenora Bell (Procurement Manager) on record; no EB/Exec Sponsor role
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped. Anthony Gambino (Manager, Supply Chain or similar procurement role per Avoma attendance) led the introductory call on 15 October 2025 but stated 'my leadership is gonna look for how much is the system' and referenced needing European management approval to formalize the project (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). This positions Gambino as an influencer or operational stakeholder, not the budget authority. The decision to invest $500k+/year will require C-level or senior VP sponsorship (likely CFO, CPO, or VP Indirect Sourcing). The account contact list includes Melissa Simpson (Director US Indirect Sourcing and Procurement), Frederick Munsch (SVP Controller and Chief Accounting Officer), and Anna Notis (Director of Finance), but none have been identified as active in this opportunity. No pricing or commercial conversation has occurred. Gap: the deal owner must map and engage the economic buyer and European management stakeholders who control the budget and project approval.

sources: Avoma call 15 October 2025, Anthony Gambino quotes and notes
decision_process.narrative: Buyer stated the process is in 'investigation stage' with no formal budget or project defined as of 15 October 2025 (source: Avoma notes). Anthony Gambino indicated that European management must approve before moving to a formal evaluation, and that leadership will benchmark against ServiceNow and SAP before committing to a new platform: 'They're gonna look to ServiceNow, and they're gonna look to SAP to say, well, what do you guys have before I go bolt on something new?' (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). A follow-up was scheduled for ~one month later (mid-November 2025) to wrap up current deals, discuss assessment options, build a business case, and decide on modules and scope (source: Avoma notes 15 Oct 2025). However, that follow-up never occurred (no subsequent calls in the manifest). The rep's 15 June 2026 Next Step note states the buyer 'still need to meet with European mgmt to see if they can move towards a formal project' — the same blocker cited 8 months earlier. No approval chain, procurement workflow, or decision timeline has been documented. Gap: the deal owner must re-engage to learn whether European management has approved the project, map the approval chain, and establish a written decision timeline.

sources: Avoma call 15 October 2025, buyer questions and Zycus responses
decision_criteria.narrative: Buyer articulated five evaluation dimensions on the 15 October 2025 call: (1) system cost and 3-year TCO, (2) implementation timeline, (3) ROI and tangible benefits vs current state, (4) fit with existing processes and change-management impact ('is it a good fit?'), and (5) competitive positioning against ServiceNow and SAP incumbent tools (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). Anthony Gambino explicitly asked: 'Do we wanna do all of it, some of it, or target it, or have additional discussion?' signaling the buyer is evaluating modular vs full-suite deployment (source: Avoma 15 Oct 2025). Tail-spend management via the Anna module was highlighted as a priority use case. However, no formal RFP, BRD, or weighted scorecard has been shared. The buyer is still in investigation mode ('quite a few pieces of the puzzle' missing, per Zycus on call). Gap: securing the formal business requirements document and decision process (RFI, RFP, or vendor shortlist criteria) is essential to validate fit and position Merlin/AI differentiation.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c on record. Buyer demonstrated AI interest on the 15 October 2025 call (asked about AI overlay, engaged on Merlin Intake natural-language interface and Anna tail-spend automation). Buyer stated they are investigating with 'limited Copilot use and Accenture AI tools' but no formal AI strategy or budget has been defined. Given the investigation-stage maturity and lack of formal project, signal is AI Curious (exploring AI but not yet committed). The 8-month silence prevents a current read; re-engagement will clarify whether AI appetite has grown or faded.

items.change: Opportunity created; introductory call held with Anthony Gambino; buyer in investigation stage, no formal budget or project defined; requested benchmarks, ROI case studies, and 3-year TCO estimate
Rep outreach via Next Step: buyer has long timeline, still awaiting European management meeting to formalize project; no buyer reply

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 15 October 2025 following an introductory call the same day. No stage progression in 255 days; remains in Qualified with a 1 December 2026 close date (158 days out). Last buyer engagement: 15 October 2025 call with Anthony Gambino (~8.5 months ago). Rep reached out 15 June 2026 noting long timeline and pending European management approval; no buyer reply in 11 days. Next_Step__c indicates buyer 'still need to meet with European mgmt to see if they can move towards a formal project' — the same gate cited on the initial call, signaling zero forward progress. LastActivityDate 24 June 2026 reflects rep-side activity, not buyer touch. No tasks, deliverables, or follow-up calls logged since October 2025.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling. Single buyer touchpoint 8+ months ago (15 October 2025) with no follow-up engagement despite promised one-month follow-up call. Rep reached out 15 June 2026 with no buyer reply in 11 days. Zero stage progression in 255 days; remains in Qualified with indefinite timeline pending European management approval (same blocker cited twice, 8 months apart). No champion, no Economic Buyer engagement, no multi-threading, and no deliverables completed (benchmarks/ROI deck promised but delivery unconfirmed). Forecast is indefensible (Pipeline forecast with no buyer engagement in 8 months). Strong signals of low internal prioritization or political stall.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 15 October 2025, close date 1 December 2026 (158 days out). Time in Qualified stage: ~255 days (8+ months) with zero stage progression. LastActivityDate 24 June 2026 is a rep-side touch (Next Step dated 15 June), not buyer engagement. Last verified buyer engagement: introductory call 15 October 2025 (~255 days ago). Submission-date series unavailable (no Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, or Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c in Q2 read). Deal has not advanced from discovery in 8+ months despite 5-month runway remaining; this is a stall, not normal enterprise pacing.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal stalled 8+ months in Qualified with no buyer engagement since October 2025 discovery call. Rep outreach mid-June unanswered. European approval and formal budget remain undefined; no advancement toward evaluation despite 158 days to close.

flags: Economic Buyer unmapped after 255 days in Qualified; budget authority (likely CFO, CPO, or VP Indirect Sourcing) must be identified and engaged to formalize project and secure $500k+/year spend approval; map EB and schedule stakeholder discovery within 14 days.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is single-threaded through an unresponsive mid-level stakeholder with no Economic Buyer mapped and no champion developed after 8+ months. European management approval gate (cited twice, 8 months apart) is the fundamental blocker preventing project formalization. Competitive positioning vs ServiceNow and SAP incumbents is unvalidated (promised benchmarks/ROI deck delivery unclear). Multi-threading to senior procurement/finance leadership and building executive sponsorship are the highest-leverage moves to restart momentum.

competitive_position.summary: ServiceNow is the primary active threat as of 15 October 2025 (most recent mention); buyer will evaluate whether ServiceNow (likely via IT/service-management) can deliver tail-spend automation before committing to Zycus. SAP is the secondary incumbent (ERP-adjacent procurement tools) also being benchmarked. No external procurement-platform competitors named. Buyer is also evaluating do-nothing / defer (no formal project yet). The competitive read is 8+ months stale; re-engagement is required to learn whether ServiceNow or SAP have proposed solutions since October 2025.

competitors.name: ServiceNow
competitors.quote: They're gonna look to ServiceNow, and they're gonna look to SAP to say, well, what do you guys have before I go bolt on something new?
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate Merlin AI / agentic intake automation delivers ROI and tail-spend fit that ServiceNow cannot match; anchor to pharma-customer benchmarks and 3-year TCO advantage; position as procurement-native vs IT-centric tooling.

competitors.name: SAP
competitors.quote: They're gonna look to ServiceNow, and they're gonna look to SAP to say, well, what do you guys have before I go bolt on something new?
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as best-of-breed procurement suite with deeper sourcing/supplier-management vs SAP's ERP-adjacent tools; emphasize Merlin AI differentiation and faster time-to-value.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Defer
competitors.quote: Investigation stage is underway with limited Copilot use and Accenture AI tools, but no formal budget or project has been defined yet.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of inaction (tail-spend leakage, manual procurement burden) and build a written ROI case; establish executive sponsorship and European management buy-in to formalize the project.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was introduced on the 15 October 2025 call (Merlin Intake demo via MS Teams natural-language interface, Anna module for tail-spend automation, strategic AI embedded across sourcing/procurement/invoicing). Buyer acknowledged AI overlay interest ('So really a lot of AI now overlay?') and engaged on tail-spend use case. However, no AIS (AI excitement) score is on record, and the Merlin/AI narrative has not been validated against buyer priorities or competitive alternatives (ServiceNow, SAP) in 8+ months. Positioning strength is unclear due to staleness; the deal owner must refresh the AI value proposition and anchor it to quantified ROI and pharmaceutical benchmarks.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expectations center on tail-spend automation, ROI validation, fit with existing processes, and competitive positioning vs ServiceNow/SAP. Zycus is aligned on tail-spend use case (Anna module demo resonated) and product scope (iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA). Partially aligned on timeline and change management (buyer needs 3-year TCO, implementation timeline, and change-impact assessment). Exposed on competitive differentiation vs incumbents (benchmarks and ROI case studies promised but delivery unconfirmed) and project formalization (European management approval still pending 8+ months later).

value.name: Lenora Bell
value.risk: Unknown engagement level; potential multi-thread target
value.title: Procurement Manager
value.sentiment: Unknown (listed as OpportunityContactRole but no engagement record)

value.name: ServiceNow
value.quote: They're gonna look to ServiceNow, and they're gonna look to SAP to say, well, what do you guys have before I go bolt on something new?
value.how_we_win: Demonstrate Merlin AI / agentic intake automation delivers ROI and tail-spend fit that ServiceNow cannot match; anchor to pharma-customer benchmarks and 3-year TCO advantage; position as procurement-native vs IT-centric tooling.

value.name: SAP
value.quote: They're gonna look to ServiceNow, and they're gonna look to SAP to say, well, what do you guys have before I go bolt on something new?
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as best-of-breed procurement suite with deeper sourcing/supplier-management vs SAP's ERP-adjacent tools; emphasize Merlin AI differentiation and faster time-to-value.

value.name: Do Nothing / Defer
value.quote: Investigation stage is underway with limited Copilot use and Accenture AI tools, but no formal budget or project has been defined yet.
value.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of inaction (tail-spend leakage, manual procurement burden) and build a written ROI case; establish executive sponsorship and European management buy-in to formalize the project.

value.detail: Buyer stated long timeline and need for European management approval before formalizing project; same gate cited on initial call (15 Oct 2025) and 8 months later (15 Jun 2026) with zero progress; timeline is indefinite until approval secured.
value.source: Avoma call 15 October 2025, Anthony Gambino; Next_Step__c 15 June 2026
